canonical.name: cache_sim
canonical.purpose: Analytical LRU-stack cache simulator (single-level, per-policy hit-rate sweep). Underscore form is used as Python identifier / dict-key; the hyphen form 'cache-sim' is its display/paper-prose sibling.

canonical.name: cache-sim
canonical.purpose: Display/paper-prose form of cache_sim. Same artifact, hyphen-styled for narrative text and matplotlib labels.

canonical.name: gem5
canonical.purpose: Cycle-accurate full-system simulator (default X86 frontend in this repo). Used as the generic gem5 key in anchor pickups; the architecture-specific siblings (gem5-riscv, gem5-x86) only appear when the run explicitly distinguishes frontends (e.g. ECG scale preflight).

canonical.name: gem5-riscv
canonical.purpose: gem5 RISC-V frontend (ECG scale runs). Distinct from the generic gem5 key because the binary path and the preflight checks differ. Result rows from RISC-V runs must carry this token, not 'gem5'.

canonical.name: gem5-x86
canonical.purpose: gem5 X86 frontend marker (cluster preflight binary label). Currently only used by ecg_cluster_preflight for human-readable diagnostic output; result rows from X86 runs still use the generic 'gem5' key.

canonical.name: sniper
canonical.purpose: Sniper interval-simulator (Pin-based instrumentation). Used as the generic Sniper key in anchor pickups; the frontend-specific sibling (sniper-sift) only appears when the run distinguishes the SIFT trace frontend.

canonical.name: sniper-sift
canonical.purpose: Sniper SIFT (Sniper Instruction Format trace) frontend marker. Used by ECG scale preflight + scale-status row tagging so trace-driven runs are distinguished from Pin-driven runs.

rules.R1: every harvested backend/tool literal is in CANONICAL_BACKENDS
rules.R2: every canonical has non-empty family ∈ {cache_sim,gem5,sniper} + non-empty paper_label
rules.R3: no two canonicals share a paper_label unless they are declared punctuation variants
rules.R4: every canonical's punctuation_variants includes its own name
rules.R7: every canonical token is referenced by at least one in-tree literal
